name: Nantua Sauce- Great Chefs
recipeInstructions:
Mirepoix is a mixture of diced onions, carrots, celery, and leeks.
1. Heat olive oil in a sauté pan over medium heat.
2. When the oil is very hot, add the live crayfish and sauté for 1 minute.
3. Add garlic bulb (halved), mirepoix, peppercorns, bay leaf, and button mushrooms. Cook for another minute.
4. Add cognac and flame the pan.
5. Pour contents into a saucepan and mash with a spoon.
6. To the saucepan with crayfish, add chopped tomatoes, parsley, tarragon, and tomato puree.
7. Deglaze the sauté pan with fish stock (enough to cover ingredients in the saucepan) and pour contents of deglazed pan into the saucepan with crayfish and vegetables.
8. Roughly mash the saucepan contents again.
9. Cook (boiling) for 20 minutes.
10. Strain the contents through a chinois into another sauté pan.
11. Reduce liquid by two-thirds.
12. Add cream, salt, pepper, and cayenne pepper. Reduce to one-half.
13. Strain and reserve.
